explanations.15: array_rand() returns the key (index number) of a random item in the array, not the item itself — that's why we use it to look up the actual word on the next line.
explanations.29: We're using the number of guesses left as an index to pick which hangman stage to display, so fewer guesses left means a more complete hangman drawing.
explanations.42: trim() removes the extra spaces we added after each letter, so the output looks clean instead of having trailing spaces.
explanations.56: fgets(STDIN) reads a line of text that the user types into the terminal, and trim() removes the newline character at the end.
explanations.58: ctype_alpha() checks if the input contains only letters — this ensures the guess is a valid letter and not a number or symbol.
explanations.72: strpos() searches for the guessed letter in the word and returns false if it's not found anywhere, which means the guess was wrong.
explanations.76: This line tries to display the word, captures its output, and then checks if all blanks have been filled in — but this is buggy because displayWord() echoes output instead of returning it.
